title: zauschneria latifolia var. viscosa
intro: Zauschneria latifolia var. viscosa, also known as sticky penstemon or sticky zauschneria, is a plant species belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Its health benefits include wound healing, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or salves to harness its medicinal properties, although caution should be exercised due to potential interactions with other medications.
